claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit); Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit)
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you're enquiring about, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible.

I have one child - my son Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex!

To help determine your eligibility, I have a few more questions.

Does Alex live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

Are you responsible for Alex's day-to-day care, such as providing for his food, clothing, and general upbringing?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I look after Alex every day - I provide his meals, clothes, and everything he needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Lovely, thank you for confirming that.

Now, a couple of questions about yourself:

Are you aged 16 or over?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. 

One more question: Do you live in the UK? And if so, are you a UK citizen or do you have the right to live here (for example, through settled status, a visa, or other immigration status)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and I'm a UK citizen.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me just check the eligibility rules to make sure I give you accurate guidance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the information you've provided and the eligibility rules, I have enough details to make a determination about your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why you qualify:

- ✅ **Alex is under 16** (he's 8 years old)
- ✅ **Alex lives with you**
- ✅ **You are responsible for his day-to-day care** (providing food, clothes, and upbringing)
- ✅ **You are over 16 years old**
- ✅ **You live in the UK and have the right to reside here** as a UK citizen

You can apply for Child Benefit through the official government channels. Just keep in mind that only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, so if anyone else is also caring for Alex, you'll need to agree between yourselves who will make the claim.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under the age of 16. The claimant lives with Alex and is responsible for his day-to-day care, including providing food, clothes, and general upbringing. The claimant is over 16 years old, lives in the UK, and is a UK citizen with the right to reside. All the core eligibility criteria for Child Benefit are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the key eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. They are over 16, live in the UK as a UK citizen, and are responsible for a child (Alex, aged 8) who lives with them. There are no disqualifying factors present - Alex is under 16, lives with the claimant, and the claimant provides for his day-to-day needs.

The claimant is over 16 years old, lives in the UK, and is a UK citizen with the right to reside. All the core eligibility criteria for Child Benefit are met for Alex.

They are over 16, live in the UK as a UK citizen, and are responsible for a child (Alex, aged 8) who lives with them. There are no disqualifying factors present - Alex is under 16, lives with the claimant, and the claimant provides for his day-to-day needs.
